data.text: RBC, other big Canadian banks beat profit estimates as economy resists tariff impact
data.snippet: Canada's six biggest banks exceeded profit forecasts this week in results boosted by wealth management and fee-based income, underlining the resilience of the country's economy to the impact of U.S. tariffs and broader policy uncertainty.

image.text: Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) logo is seen in this illustration taken December 22, 2023.

relatedHeadlines.text: Canada's RBC beats profit estimates on retail banking, wealth management boom
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Royal Bank of Canada beat analysts' estimates for first-quarter profit on Thursday, supported by strength in its wealth management and personal banking segments.

relatedHeadlines.text: Canada's BMO beats profit estimate on credit improvement
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Canadian bank BMO Financial beat analysts' estimates for first-quarter profit, after it set aside less money than expected to cover potential loan defaults, sending shares to a record high on Wednesday.

relatedHeadlines.text: National Bank of Canada's profit gets a lift from Canadian Western deal
relatedHeadlines.snippet: National Bank of Canada reported higher first-quarter profit on Wednesday, as the acquisition of Canadian Western Bank boosted its personal and commercial banking business.

data.text: CIBC quarterly profit jumps on capital markets strength
data.snippet: Canadian lender CIBC reported a jump in first-quarter profit on Thursday, buoyed by robust performance in its capital markets business.

image.text: CIBC logo is seen in this illustration taken January 7, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: Canada's Scotiabank beats profit estimates, expects to hit key target ahead of plan
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Canadian lender Bank of Nova Scotia on Tuesday reported quarterly earnings that beat analysts' estimates, supported by strong growth across segments, and said it would likely hit a key earnings target a year ahead of schedule.

data.text: Toronto Dominion Bank's quarterly profit rises on higher interest income
data.snippet: Toronto Dominion Bank reported a rise in first-quarter profit on Thursday, helped by higher income from interest, as Canadian lenders continue to be resilient amid ongoing trade friction with the United States.

image.text: TD BANK logo is seen in this illustration taken January 7, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: Scotiabank says Mexico operations stable, no impact from violence
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Bank of Nova Scotia executives said on Tuesday the Canadian lender's operations in Mexico were stable and had seen no impact on business after unrest triggered by the killing of cartel leader Nemesio Oseguera, known as "El Mencho," in a military raid.

data.text: Canada says it could have bilateral critical sector deals with US
data.snippet: Canada is negotiating with the U.S. to remove tariffs on some critical sectors, and a deal could be wrapped into bilateral pacts alongside a review of the United States-Mexico-Canada free trade agreement, a Canadian senior minister said on Thursday.

image.text: Canada's Minister responsible for Canada–U.S. Trade and Intergovernmental Affairs and One Canadian Economy, Dominic LeBlanc, speaks as he and Mexico's Economy Minister Marcelo Ebrard (not pictured) attend the kickoff of the Team Canada Trade Mission to Mexico, at a hotel in Mexico City, Mexico, February 16, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: US, Canada trade teams may meet soon, officials say
relatedHeadlines.snippet: U.S. and Canadian trade officials spoke on Wednesday and plan to meet in coming weeks, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said, adding that the Trump administration was open to their ideas on how to reach an agreement.

data.text: Bombardier has chance to boost business jet sales in India, says CEO
data.snippet: India's efforts to improve its airport infrastructure will give Bombardier a chance to boost sales of business jets there, chief executive officer Eric Martel said on Thursday.

image.text: A view shows Bombardier's private jet completion center in Montreal, Quebec, Canada February 6, 2025.

data.text: CANADA STOCKS-TSX falls as weakness in commodities offsets gains in tech stocks
data.snippet: Canada's resources-heavy index fell on Thursday as weakness in commodity-linked stocks outweighed gains in tech stocks driven by upbeat earnings from Salesforce.

image.text: The Art Deco facade of the original Toronto Stock Exchange building is seen on Bay Street in Toronto, Ontario, Canada January 23, 2019.

relatedHeadlines.text: POLL-Canada's TSX seen hitting new highs on sector rotation
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Canada's main stock index is expected to post additional record highs this year, supported by elevated commodity prices and the increased attractiveness of companies in established industries that could benefit from an upswing in the global economy, a Reuters poll found.

data.text: CANADA FX DEBT-Canadian dollar edges lower on flight to safety
data.snippet: The Canadian dollar edged lower against its U.S. counterpart on Thursday, as safe-haven demand for the greenback offset domestic data that showed a sharp narrowing in Canada's current account deficit.

image.text: A Canadian Loonie, otherwise known as a one dollar coin, is displayed on top of an American one dollar bill in this posed photograph in Toronto, October 10, 2008.

notifications.message: Your requested language with value en-GB is not supported, using en-us instead.
